Ultimately, their bond is not one of trust or affection, but of necessity—survival demands cooperation despite unresolved conflict.

Their final interactions suggest a grudging acceptance, though the undercurrent of doubt remains, particularly in Call’s bitter commentary on Ripley’s humanity.
